info.title: Appen AI Data Annotation Platform (ADAP) API
info.description:
# Introduction

The Appen AI Data Annotation Platform (ADAP) APIs offer a structured, automated approach to ensure high-quality data labeling on a scale. These APIs facilitate seamless integration of quality assurance steps, including reviewer assignments, and performance scoring, into annotation workflows. 

By standardizing quality processes and enabling programmatic control, they reduce errors, improve data consistency, and provide reliable training data for AI and machine learning models. 

Before integrating with the API, we recommend building and running a job using our **[graphical user interface](https://client.appen.com/sessions/new)**. Once you successfully run a job and are satisfied with the results, you can automate the process using the Appen API.

# Authentication

To authenticate and access the API, follow these steps to find it:

**Obtain Your API Key:**

- Click your profile picture in the top right corner of any page, then select Profile from the dropdown.

![](https://your-bucket-name.s3.amazonaws.com/images/profile-picture-location.png)   
![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/authentication1.png)   

- Go to the API tab and click Generate New to create an API key.

![](https://your-bucket-name.s3.amazonaws.com/images/api-tab-location.png)    
![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/APIKey-Screenshot.png)    

**Note:** Creating a new API key will invalidate any previous keys for this user, so update any code that uses the old key.

- For requests listed below, the API Key will be listed as `{api_key}`.    

- Include the API key in the header for each request as follows:

```
headers: { 

  "AUTHORIZATION": "Token token={api_key}"

}
```

**Other Variables:**

- `{job_id}` and `{team_id}` can be found on the job listing page or the Teams tab URL on the Teams Tab Account page.

![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/TeamId.png)

![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ADAP/jobId.png)

# Resources

There are four primary data objects on Appen that correspond to resources on the API:

## Projects

A **Project** is a container that organizes and structures your data annotation workflows. 

Projects serve as the foundation for organizing related jobs, units, and data within your team. 

Each project belongs to a specific team and can contain multiple jobs.

Projects have unique names that help you organize and identify different annotation initiatives. Think of a project as a workspace where you can group related annotation tasks, manage data uploads, and coordinate quality assurance workflows across multiple jobs.


## Jobs

A **Job** is an interface that connects your data to an online workforce. Each job on the Appen platform has data, instructions, customizable questions for your use case (written in CML), [test questions](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202702985-Guide-to-Creating-Test-Questions), and is worked on by Contributors. Contributors submit Judgments on the rows of data via a worker interface.

- All jobs in a single account can be found on your project canvas page and will be identified by a unique numeric id.

## Units (also known as 'Rows')

A **Unit** of data is uploaded to the job from your source data file via API or GUI.

## Judgments

A **Judgment** is the set of responses submitted by a contributor on a particular unit of data. It is recommended to collect multiple judgments and compare them to one another or aggregate to the top response. For each job, you can specify the number of judgments you would like each row to receive.

# Responses and Messaging

Each request to the Appen API returns an HTTP status code response and one or both of the following:

- An application response message (in JSON)
- A JSON representation of the object (job, unit, or judgments) requested See the below sections of different API Requests, Responses and Messaging for more information:

1. **[Project Creation](#operation/createProject)**
2. **[Upload Dataset](#operation/uploadDatasetToProject)**
3. **[Upload Dataset Status](#operation/getUploadStatus)**
4. **[Job Creation](#operation/createJob)**
5. **[Copy Job](#operation/copyJob)**
6. **[Job Status](#operation/getJobStatus)**
7. **[Update Job Settings](#operation/updateJob)**
8. **[List Units in the Project](#operation/listProjectUnits)**
9. **[Send Units to the Job](#operation/sendUnitsToJob)**
10. **[Launch & Resume/Run job](#operation/launchJob)**
11. **[Pause Job](#operation/pauseJob)**
12. **[Download Reports](#operation/downloadCsvFile)**

post.description:
Initializes a new job within a project. Many fields are auto-populated and cannot be overridden.

**Supported Job Types:**
- **WORK**: Standard work jobs for data collection and annotation
- **QA**: Quality assurance/Leading QA jobs

**Current Limitations:**
- Work Jobs requiring Ontology and qualification jobs will be supported in next version of the release
- By default the created Jobs will be in 'Draft' status
- Job type must be either WORK or QA (other types not supported)

x-codeSamples.label: Create Job
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.appen.com/v3/jobs/jobs.json" \
  -H "Authorization: Token token=<your-api-key>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "title": "Your Job Title",
    "projectId": "e74c1b9d-16ff-4a29-8c4c-e3147877de1b",
    "type": "WORK"
  }'

title.description: Title for the new job - must be unique within the project (required)

projectId.description: ID of the project where the job will be created (required)

type.description: Job type - must be either WORK or QA (required)

400.description: Bad request - Missing required fields or validation errors

post.summary: Copy an existing job within a project
post.description:
Creates a copy of an existing job within the same project.
This endpoint allows you to duplicate job configurations including design, contributor settings, 
and row settings, making it easy to create similar jobs without starting from scratch.

**What gets copied:**
- Basic job metadata (title, type, description)
- Job design/CML configuration (if `DESIGN` is included in `types`)
- Contributor settings (if `CROWD_SETTINGS` is included in `types`)
- Row settings and pricing configuration (if `PRICE_AND_ROW` is included in `types`)
- Quality/Leading QA settings (if `QUALITY_SETTINGS` is included in `types`)

**Important Notes:**
- The copied job will be created in `DRAFT` status
- Jobs can only be copied within the same project (source job and target project must match)
- The job title must be unique within the project
- The `appendTo` field must be set to `project_data_source`

**Limitations**
- Only WORK jobs and Leading QA jobs can be copied. Copying of following QA jobs will be supported in the further releases. 

**Validation:**
- Only allowed values for `types`: `DESIGN`, `CROWD_SETTINGS`, `PRICE_AND_ROW`, `QUALITY_SETTINGS`
- `QUALITY_SETTINGS` can only be used when copying from a QA/Leading QA job (not WORK jobs)

x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.appen.com/v3/project-flow/projects/jobs/copy-job?teamId=f6c0b8cb-de69-495c-980b-2b0e0a6ca288" \
  -H "Authorization: Token token=<your-api-key>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "copiedFrom": "63311198-5481-4312-ae33-f9fcf1423191",
    "projectId": "fbff3f10-7105-4e52-afef-00fe21a3100e",
    "title": "Copy of My Work Job",
    "appendTo": "project_data_source",
    "types": ["DESIGN", "CROWD_SETTINGS", "PRICE_AND_ROW"]
  }'

requestBody.description:
Job copy configuration specifying the source job, target project, new job title, 
and which components to copy.

copiedFrom.description: ID of the source job to copy from (required)

projectId.description: ID of the target project where the job will be created (required)

title.description: Title for the new job - must be unique within the target project (required)

types.description:
Components to copy from the source job. Leave empty to copy basic structure only.
- **DESIGN**: Copy job design/CML configuration
- **CROWD_SETTINGS**: Copy contributor settings
- **PRICE_AND_ROW**: Copy row settings and pricing configuration
- **QUALITY_SETTINGS**: Copy quality/leading QA settings

get.description:
Retrieves the current status and progress metrics for a specific job.

This endpoint provides comprehensive job progress information including:
- **Completion percentage**: Overall job completion progress (0-100%)
- **Unit statistics**: Total units and submitted units in the job
- **Judgment statistics**: Total and submitted judgments for the job
- **Test questions**: Total available test questions for the job

**Supported Job Types:**
- WORK jobs
- QA jobs

x-codeSamples.label: Get Job Status
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X GET "https://api.appen.com/v3/project-flow/projects/jobs/status?teamId=cf22e84f-06c8-4955-b626-1c47d245a176&jobId=db66f1e3-7260-45a8-b8c6-9359bbdbfd3f" \
  -H "Authorization: Token token=<your-api-key>"

parameters.description: The unique identifier for your team

parameters.name: jobId

submittedUnits.description: Number of units that have been submitted/finalized

totalJudgments.description: Total number of judgments expected for the job

submittedJudgments.description: Number of judgments that have been submitted (COMMITTED status)

totalTestQuestions.description: Total number of available test questions for the job

put.description:
Updates job settings for the specified team. This endpoint allows you to modify various job properties
including basic settings, validation rules, expiration settings, and dynamic judgement configuration.

## Mandatory Fields
The following fields are **mandatory** and must be provided in every update request:
- `id` - The unique job identifier
- `projectId` - Project identifier this job belongs to  
- `teamId` - Team identifier that owns the job
- `title` - Job title (must be unique within the project)
- `type` - Job type (WORK or QA)

## Necessary Fields
The following are important fields that must be updated in every job settings
- `assignmentLeaseExpiry` - Assignment lease in seconds
- `judgmentsPerRow` - Judgments per row
- `rowsPerPage` - Rows per page
- `enableShareLink` - Must be set to true to retrieve the job's `internalSecret`

## Optional
- Omitting a field leaves the current value unchanged
- `timeLimitThresholdSec`: 0 (disabled)
- `enableExpirationTime`: 0 (disabled)
- `expirationDurationSet`: 0 (disabled)
- `expirationDurationUpdate`: 0 (disabled)
- `dynamicJudgements.minimumConfidence`: 0.0 (disabled)
- `dynamicJudgements.maxDynamicJudgements`: 0
- Counts (e.g., maxJudgmentPerContributor, maxNumberOfAllowedAbandonment): 0 allowed (non-negative)

## Validations
- Team ID: Body `teamId` must match query `teamId`
- Title: Must be unique within project (duplicate → error 60080)
- Assignment lease expiry: integer seconds, minimum 60
- Judgments per row: integer ≥ 1; Rows per page: integer ≥ 1
- Time limit threshold (`timeLimitThresholdSec`): percentage 0–100 (0 = disabled)
- Expiration: `enableExpirationTime` (0 = disabled, > 0 = enabled). When enabled, `expirationDurationSet` and
  `expirationDurationUpdate` must be > 0. All expiration fields accept 0 (disabled)
- Dynamic judgements: `minimumConfidence` 0.0–1.0 (0 = disabled). Mode MINIMUM_CONFIDENCE requires
  `minimumConfidence`; mode MATCHING_JUDGEMENTS requires `matchingJudgements`. Do not provide both
- Enable Share Link: boolean; when true, shareable link generation is enabled for the job
- CML (if provided in `jobCml.cml`): must contain at least one `<cml:*>` element; payload < 2MB; disallows
  `<script>`/`javascript:`; attribute values must be quoted. When `dynamicJudgements.enableDynamicJudgements = true`,
  at least one labeled CML question (`label` attribute) is required. When false, labels are not required. If embedding
  CML in JSON, escape quotes (") and backslashes (\\)

## Limitations
- Settings related to Test Questions, Management of Internal/External contributors, CSS and JS for CML design will be supported in next version of the release

Common errors:
- 400: Missing required fields, invalid values, or mismatched teamId.
- 60080: Job title already exists in project.
- 60000: Job not found or access denied.
- 500: Internal error (ensure required fields are present and valid).

get.description:
Returns the current job status and core metadata for the given `jobId`.
Intended for dashboards and polling; returns a lightweight subset of `JobDTO` (for example:
`id`, `title`, `type`, `state`, `activeStatus`, `assignmentLeaseExpiry`, `rowsPerPage`,
`judgmentsPerRow`, `displayId`, `teamId`, `createdAt`) and omits large assets/collections.

  -H "Authorization: Token token=<your-api-key>"

post.description:
Temporarily stops a running job, pausing all new contributor assignments while preserving current progress. This allows you to halt work without losing completed annotations.

**What Happens When You Pause:**
- Job status changes from DRAFT to PAUSED
- Contributors cannot start new assignments
- No new units are distributed to contributors
- All progress and judgments are preserved

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Job identifier to pause

x-codeSamples.label: Pause Job
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.appen.com/v3/jobs/pause?jobId=<jobId>" \
  -H "Authorization: Token token=<your-api-key>"

400.description: Bad request - Invalid job ID or job already paused

value.message: Invalid job ID
Job already paused

post.summary: Upload unit data to an existing project
post.description:
Uploads a CSV file to an existing project. The API requires `projectId` and `fileName` as query parameters. Returns upload status and file metadata, including a status polling URL.

**Supported Data Types:**
- **Units**: Individual data rows for annotation tasks
- **Unit Groups**: Collections of related units that can be processed together

**Date Format Support:**
Only CSV files with following date formats are supported:
- `yyyy/MM/dd` (e.g., 2025/03/14)
- `yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss` (e.g., 2025/03/14 07:52:46)
- `yyyy-MM-dd` (e.g., 2025-03-14)
- `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss` (e.g., 2025-03-14T07:52:46)

The status of the upload can be checked by triggering the **[Get the status of an uploaded file/dataset API](#operation/getUploadStatus)**.

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: Project identifier

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: Name of the file uploaded

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.appen.com/v3/jobs/upload.json?projectId=<projectId>&fileName=<fileName>" \
-H "Authorization: Token token=<your-api-key>" \
-H "Content-Type: text/csv" \
-T "@/path/to/your/file.csv"

post.description:
Creates a new project within a team. Project is a container that organizes and structures your data annotation workflows.
Projects serve as the foundation for organizing related jobs, units, and data within your team.
Each project belongs to a specific team and can contain multiple jobs.
Projects have unique names that help you organize and identify different annotation initiatives. 
Think of a project as a workspace where you can group related annotation tasks, manage data uploads, 
and coordinate quality assurance workflows across multiple jobs.

**Required Parameters:**
- `teamId`: The unique identifier of the team that will own this project
- `name`: A unique project name (must not duplicate existing project names in your team)

**Optional Parameters:**
- `description`: Additional details about the project purpose or scope

post.description:
Copies an existing project into the specified team. The new project name must be provided.

Note:
- The jobs inside the copied project are expected to be in draft state.
- The sample configuration in the job canvas is carried over from the origin project and QA quality settings are added by default.
- Contributor details will not be added to the Jobcrowd of copied project.

Validations applied by the Public API:
- teamId: The ID must be a valid UUID, desired destination team that must correspond to an existing, accessible team.
- originId: The ID must be a valid UUID, and the source project (the one being copied) must belong to an existing team that you have access to.
- datasetType: If omitted or blank, defaults to `NO_ROWS`; only documented enum values are accepted.

parameters.name: teamId
parameters.description: TeamId of the destination team where the copied project will be created (i.e., the team the new project will belong to). The teamId must exist; otherwise the request fails with "invalid teamId" (400).

originId.description: ID of the project to copy from (required)

datasetType.description:
Optional dataset type to attach during copy. Defaults to NO_ROWS if omitted or blank.
Allowed values: TEST_QUESTION, NEW_UNITS, ALL_UNITS, NO_ROWS.
Semantics:
  - **TEST_QUESTION**: copy only test question units
  - **NEW_UNITS**: copy units with no active segments and their related test questions
  - **ALL_UNITS**: copy all units (including test questions)
  - **NO_ROWS**: copy project structure only (no data)

isJobSettingCopyRequired.description: Optional; whether to copy job-level configuration/settings. Defaults to false.

post.description:
Sends units from a project to a specific job. You can send specific units by ID or a percentage of units, with options to carry judgments and handle conflicts.

## Request Body Parameters

- **projectId** (required): The source project identifier
- **unitIds** (optional): List of specific unit IDs to send. Omit if using `sendEntireGroup: true` to send all units in the project. 
  **Note**: You can obtain unitIds from the [List units in a project](#/paths/~1v3~1project-flow~1projects~1units/post) API response. Each unit in the response contains a `unitId` field that can be extracted and used here.
- **percentage** (optional): Percent of units to send (1-100). Used when sending a subset of units
- **sendEntireGroup** (optional): If `true`, sends all units in the unit groups to which the specified units belong to. When `true`, `unitIds` is ignored
- **carryJudgment** (optional): If `true`, existing judgments from previous jobs (e.g., work job) will be carried over to the target job (e.g., QA job). This is useful for routing judgments from work to QA jobs.
- **overwriteJudgment** (optional): If `true`, the target job (e.g., QA job) can edit/modify the carried judgments. If `false`, judgments are read-only.

## Use Cases

- **Work to QA Routing**: Set `carryJudgment: true` and `overwriteJudgment: true` to route units with their judgments from a work job to a QA job where QA can review and modify the judgments
- **Data Transfer**: Set `carryJudgment: false` to send units without any existing judgment data
- **Read-only Review**: Set `carryJudgment: true` and `overwriteJudgment: false` to allow viewing but not editing of carried judgments

## Related APIs

- **List units in a project**: Use the [List units in a project](#/paths/~1v3~1project-flow~1projects~1units/post) API to retrieve available units and extract their `unitId` values for use in this endpoint.

**Note:**
- Invoke ‘Pause Job’ API at first before actually sending units to Job. The units can be retrieved using the ‘List units in a project’ API

post.description:
Activates a previously created job, making it available for contributors to work on. This transitions the job from PAUSE to RUNNING state and begins the actual annotation process.



# Contributor Access URL Construction

With this release, customers can now generate the Contributor Access URL programmatically.

### URL Structure
```
https://account.appen.com/quality/tasks/{jobId}?secret={secret}
```

### Components
- **Base URL**: `https://account.appen.com/quality/tasks/` (constant string)
- **Job ID**: Returned in the response of the Create Job API (example: `2d215241-00bc-4453-bac3-449387a78190`)
- **Secret**: Returned in the response of the Update Job Settings API (example: `xicPrChRfj28e7GLZiDykuTDQZqpJYv6QkBRLacU5bp4eE`)

### Example Construction
```
https://account.appen.com/quality/tasks/2d215241-00bc-4453-bac3-449387a78190?secret=xicPrChRfj28e7GLZiDykuTDQZqpJYv6QkBRLacU5bp4eE
```


**Limitations:**
- Jobs can be launched only in ‘Work’ mode but NOT in either ‘Quiz’ mode / ‘Quiz+Work’ mode. Test Question feature will be supported in the subsequent releases,then the Jobs can be launched in all modes.
- Management of Internal/External contributors through crowd type will be supported in the subsequent releases.

post.description:
**Download a report from a job/project.**

- **Job-level reports**: Use `jobId` parameter to download reports for a specific job.
- **Project-level reports**: Use `projectId` parameter to download reports for an entire project.
- **Aggregated reports**: Use request body to specify aggregation settings.

- **Either `jobId` or `projectId` must be provided, but not both.**

**Response Behavior:**
- **202 Response**: Report download is in progress. **You must call this same endpoint again with the exact same query parameters** (`type`, `filename`, `jobId` or `projectId`) to check the status. Repeat the request every 2-5 seconds until you receive a 200 response.
- **200 Response**: Report is ready and is downloaded to the path where the curl is executed. The file content is streamed directly in the response body. **No separate URL fetch is needed** - the file is streamed directly when ready.

**Note**: 
- **curl users**: Files are automatically saved using the `-o` flag
- **Postman/API tool users**: Need to use the "Save response to file" option like below

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the job to download the report for.

**This 'jobId' parameter must be only used when downloading Job Level Reports**

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the project to download the report for.

**This 'projectId' parameter must be only used when downloading Project Level Reports**

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Specifies the type of report to generate or download. Valid values include:


**Job-level report types:**
- **full** – Returns the Full report containing every judgment.
- **json** – Returns the JSON report containing both aggregated responses and individual judgments.
- **aggregated** – Returns the Aggregated report with custom aggregation settings (requires request body).
- **workset** – Returns the Contributor (Workset) report.
- **source** – Returns a CSV of the source data uploaded to the job.
- **ai_detector_report_contributor_level** - Returns an AI Detector Report at Contributor Level
- **ai_detector_report_unit_level** - Returns an AI Detector Report at Unit Level
- **test_question_report_contributor_level** - Returns Test Question Report at Contributor Level
- **gold_report** - Returns Test Question Report with selected or all units
- **k_alpha_report** - Returns Inter Annotator Agreement (K - Alpha) Report
- **contributor_scores_report** - Returns the Contributor Scores Report.

** **

**Project-level report types:**
- **job_daily_report_project_level** – Returns the Job Daily report for the entire project
- **contributor_report_project_level** – Returns the Contributor report for the entire project
- **source_report_project_level** – Returns the Source report for the entire project
- **json_report_project_level** – Returns the JSON report for the entire project

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The name of the output file you would like to specify

requestBody.description:
Request body settings for different report types.

**For `type=aggregated`**: Aggregated results settings that allow you to specify how multiple contributors judgments will be summarized in the aggregated report of a job.

**For `type=gold_report`**: Test question report settings that allow you to specify which units to include in the test question report.


**📋 Required Configuration:**
You MUST select one aggregation algorithm for EACH judgment column in the request body. For example:
- Select one algorithm for `_meta` column
- Select one algorithm for `ask_question_here` column

**Available Algorithms:**
- **AGG** (Best Answer) 
- **AGG_X** (Top # Answers) 
- **CAGG_X** (Confidence Score)
- **ALL** (All Answers)
- **AVG** (Numeric Average) 

**⚠️ Parameter Requirements:**
- If you select **AGG_X**, you MUST specify `numberOfAnswers` parameter
- If you select **CAGG_X**, you MUST specify `minimumConfidence` parameter. **minimumConfidence** value should range between 0 and 1.
- For **AGG**, **ALL**, and **AVG**, no parameters are needed

judgmentColumnAggregation.description: Array of aggregation settings for each judgment column. You MUST include ALL judgment columns in the request body, each with its own aggregation algorithm.

judgmentColumn.description: The name of the judgment column to aggregate. Must be one of the judgment columns from your job (e.g., "_meta", "ask_question_here")

aggregationAlgorithm.description: The aggregation algorithm to use for this column

parameter.description: Parameters for the aggregation algorithm (**REQUIRED for AGG_X and CAGG_X, NOT NEEDED for AGG, ALL, AVG**)

numberOfAnswers.description: **REQUIRED for AGG_X**: Number of top answers to include (positive integer)

minimumConfidence.description: **REQUIRED for CAGG_X**: Minimum confidence threshold between 0.0 and 1.0

oneOf.description: Request body for test question reports (type=gold_report)

addTestQuestionVO.description: Array of test question units to include in the report. Required when `type=gold_report`. Use empty array `[]` to include all units.

unitId.description: The unique identifier of the unit

unitDisplayId.description: The display identifier of the unit

post.description:
Download dataset from a project with customizable unit selection, column filtering, and filter support. 

**Dataset Types**
- **DATA_SET** - Unit View Dataset
- **JUDGMENT_DATA_SET** - Judgement View Dataset

**Features**
- **Unit Selection**: Specify specific unit IDs or leave empty to download all units
- **Column Filtering**: Specify only the required columns
- **Filter Support**: Apply saved filters created in the UI using filterName and userId/akonId

**Response Behavior:**
- **202 Response**: Dataset download is in progress. **You must call this same endpoint again with the exact same query parameters** (`type`, `filename`, `projectId`, and optionally `filterName`, `userId`) to check the status. Repeat the request every 2-5 seconds until you receive a 200 response.
- **200 Response**: Dataset is ready and is downloaded to the path where the curl is executed. The file content is streamed directly in the response body. **No separate URL fetch is needed** - the file is streamed directly when ready.

**Note**: 
- **curl users**: Files are automatically saved using the `-o` flag
- **Postman/API tool users**: Need to use the "Save response to file" option like below

parameters.name: projectId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the project to download the dataset from.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Specifies the type of dataset to download. Valid values include:

- **DATA_SET** – Unit View dataset
- **JUDGMENT_DATA_SET** – Judgment View dataset

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The name of the output file you would like to specify

parameters.name: filterName
parameters.description: Optional filter name, requires userId/akonId.

parameters.name: userId/akonId
parameters.description: AKON ID of the user. Required when using filterName.

requestBody.description:
Dataset download configuration specifying which units and columns to include.

**Unit Selection:**
- Provide specific unit IDs in the `unitIds` array to download only those units
- Leave `unitIds` array empty to download all units from the project

**Column Selection:**
- Provide specific column names in the `columns` array to include only those columns
- Leave `columns` array empty to include all available columns

# Available Columns:

**Unit View (DATA_SET)**

| Column Name | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| unitDisplayId | Unique identifier of the unit (display format). |
| replica | Replica identifier. |
| datasetRowType | Type of dataset row. |
| segmentGroupId | Identifier of the segment group. |
| jobTitle | Title of the associated job. |
| jobAlias | Alias of the job. |
| jobJudgmentsPerRow | Number of judgments required per row. |
| jobStatus | Status of the job for the unit. |
| jobType | Type of the job. |
| sourceFileBatchNumber | Batch number of the source file. |
| rowNumber | Row number in the dataset. |
| unitTagName | Tag name of the unit. |
| unitSourceMd5 | MD5 hash of the source. |
| createdAt | Timestamp when the unit was created. |
| judgmentsCollected | Number of collected judgments. |
| unitSegmentType | Type of unit segment. |
| questionId | ID of the associated question. |
| source._UNIT_GROUP | Unit group information. |
| source.audio_annotation | Audio annotation source. |
| source.audio_name | Name of the audio source. |
| source.audio_url | URL of the audio source. |

**Judgment View (JUDGMENT_DATA_SET)**

| Column Name | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| latest.jobTitle | Title of the latest job cycle. |
| latest.jobAlias | Alias of the latest job cycle. |
| latest.jobJudgmentsPerRow | Required judgments per row in the latest cycle. |
| latest.jobCycleNumber | Cycle number of the latest job. |
| latest.jobStatus | Status of the job in the latest cycle. |
| latest.jobType | Type of the latest job cycle. |
| latest.workerEmail | Email of the worker. |
| latest.workerExternalId | External ID of the worker. |
| latest.workerId | Worker ID. |
| latest.workerGender | Gender of the worker. |
| latest.workerLanguage | Language of the worker. |
| latest.lastAnnotatorEmail | Email of the last annotator. |
| latest.lastAnnotatorExternalId | External ID of the last annotator. |
| latest.lastAnnotator | Name of the last annotator. |
| latest.activeStatus | Worker's active status. |
| latest.agreementScore | Agreement score for the judgment. |
| latest.committedAt | Timestamp when committed. |
| latest.createdAt | Timestamp when created. |
| latest.distributionStatus | Distribution status of the judgment. |
| latest.expiresAt | Expiry timestamp. |
| latest.flowStatus | Flow status of the judgment. |
| latest.judgment.{output_field_name} | Output fields defined in job design. |
| latest.judgmentRawJson | Raw JSON judgment data. |
| latest.judgmentsCollected | Number of collected judgments. |
| latest.qualityScore | Quality score of the judgment. |
| latest.replica | Replica identifier. |
| latest.replicaGroupId | Replica group identifier. |
| latest.reservedAt | Timestamp when reserved. |
| latest.segmentSequence | Segment sequence identifier. |
| latest.updatedAt | Timestamp when updated. |
| earliest.* | Same fields as latest.* but for the earliest judgment. |
| history.* | Same fields as latest.* but for all judgment history records. |

**Notes**

- **latest.judgment.{output_field_name}** – Generated Columns: For each output field defined in the job design, a corresponding column in the format latest.judgment.{output_field_name} is automatically generated. These can also be explicitly included in the columns array when invoking the Dataset Download API.

## Creating and Using Filters

### Create and Apply a Filter in the Dataset UI
![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.amazonaws.com/adap/CreateFilter.png)

### Save the Filter with a Name
![](https://qualityflow-api-docs.s3.amazonaws.com/adap/SaveFilter.png)

Once saved, the filter name can be passed in the request or cURL command to download filtered datasets with selected columns.

**Note**: Filter creation is supported only in the QF Dataset UI Page.

post.description:
Update existing test questions and their settings within a job. This endpoint allows you to modify test question content, answers, explanations, and operational modes.

**Important Notes**: 
1. Only existing test questions can be updated. The unitId must correspond to an existing test question in the job.
2. All jobIds must belong to the specified project.

JobDTO.description:
Job Data Transfer Object for updating job settings.

## FIELD OVERVIEW

** REQUIRED (5):** `id`, `projectId`, `teamId`, `title`, `type`

** CORE CONFIG:** `version`, `assignmentLeaseExpiry`, `judgmentsPerRow`, `rowsPerPage`, `displayContributorName`, `judgmentModifiable`

** WORKFLOW:** `state`, `activeStatus`, `feedback`, `sendBackOp`, `unitSegmentType`, `allowSelfQa`, `allowAbandonUnits`

** TIME & LIMITS:** `timeLimitThresholdSec` (0-100%), `enableExpirationTime`, `expirationDurationSet`, `expirationDurationUpdate`

** QUALITY:** `dynamicJudgements` (minimumConfidence 0.0-1.0), `threshold` (0.0-1.0), `maxJudgmentPerContributor`

** FEATURES:** `enableBlueTooth`, `enableShareLink`, `enableExternalAutoAssignJob`, `launchPlatform`, `latestAppVersion`

** SESSION:** `totalSessionNumber`, `reusablePrompt`, `reusablePin`, `maxSkippablePromptNum`

** CONTENT:** `instruction`, `jobCml`, `jobFilter`, `jobCrowd`, `testQuestionSettings`

** BUSINESS:** `payRateType`, `invoiceStatisticsType`, `validationRuleId`, `qaAdvancedOptions`

** METADATA:** `createdAt`, `displayId`, `flowId`, `internalSecret`, `copiedFrom` (read-only fields)

## KEY RULES
- TeamId must match query parameter | Job titles must be unique in project
- Negative values not allowed for: time, expiration, confidence fields
- timeLimitThresholdSec is percentage (0-100), not seconds
- When enableExpirationTime > 0, duration fields must be > 0
- WORK jobs cannot have feedback ENABLED

id.description: **REQUIRED**: Unique job identifier (UUID). Identifies the job to update.

version.description: **RECOMMENDED**: Job version for optimistic locking. Prevents conflicting updates.

projectId.description: **REQUIRED**: Project identifier (UUID). Must have access to this project.

teamId.description: **REQUIRED**: Team identifier (UUID). Must match teamId query parameter.

title.description: **REQUIRED**: Job title. Must be unique within project (error 60080 if duplicate).

displayId.description: **READ-ONLY**: Auto-generated human-readable job ID. Default: auto-generated.

feedback.description: **OPTIONAL**: Contributor feedback setting. Default: DISABLED. WORK jobs cannot have ENABLED.

flowId.description: **OPTIONAL**: Workflow identifier (UUID). Default: same as job ID.

type.description: **REQUIRED**: Job type. WORK = standard job, QA = quality assurance. WORK jobs cannot have feedback ENABLED.

state.description: **OPTIONAL**: Current job state. Controls job execution status.

assignmentLeaseExpiry.description: **RECOMMENDED**: Assignment lease time in seconds. Min: 60, controls how long assignments are held.

judgmentsPerRow.description: **RECOMMENDED**: Judgments required per row. Default: 1, Min: 1. Higher = more consensus.

rowsPerPage.description: **RECOMMENDED**: Rows per assignment page. Min: 1, controls assignment size.

judgmentModifiable.description: **OPTIONAL**: Allow judgment editing after submission. Default: false.

copiedFrom.description:
**READ-ONLY**: ID of job this was copied from (if applicable).

- **Default**: null
- **Purpose**: Track job lineage for copied jobs
- **Note**: Set automatically when copying jobs

maxJudgmentPerContributorEnabled.description:
**OPTIONAL**: Whether to limit judgments per contributor.

- **Default**: false
- **Purpose**: Enable contributor workload limits
- **Related**: Works with maxJudgmentPerContributor field

maxJudgmentPerContributor.description:
**OPTIONAL**: Maximum judgments per contributor.

- **Default**: 0 (unlimited)
- **Range**: ≥ 0 (0 = unlimited, positive values set limit)
- **Purpose**: Prevent single contributor from dominating job
- **Requirement**: Only applies when maxJudgmentPerContributorEnabled = true

maxSkippablePromptNum.description:
**OPTIONAL**: Maximum number of skippable prompts.

- **Default**: 0 (no skipping allowed)
- **Range**: ≥ 0 (0 = no skipping, positive values allow skipping)
- **Purpose**: Allow contributors to skip difficult assignments

unitSegmentType.description:
**OPTIONAL**: Unit segment type for data presentation.

- **Default**: UNIT_ONLY
- **Values**:
  - UNIT_ONLY: Present data as single units
  - UNIT_SEGMENT: Present data in segments
  - UNIT_WITH_PREVIEW: Include preview capability
- **Purpose**: Controls how data is structured for contributors

internalSecret.description:
**SYSTEM**: Internal secret for job access (auto-generated).

- **Default**: Auto-generated secure string
- **Purpose**: Internal authentication token
- **Note**: Do not modify unless specifically required

maxNumberOfAllowedAbandonment.description: Maximum number of allowed abandonments (must be non-negative)

sendAbandonedUnitsBackToJob.description: Whether abandoned units are sent back to job

latestAppVersion.description: Whether latest app version is required

totalSessionNumber.description: Total number of sessions (0 = no limit, positive values set session limit)

appendable.description: **OPTIONAL**: Allow job data appending. Default: false.

enableShareLink.description: **OPTIONAL**: Enable job sharing links. Default: false.

payRateType.description: **OPTIONAL**: Payment rate type. Default: empty string.

invoiceStatisticsType.description: **OPTIONAL**: Invoice statistics calculation type. Default: UNIT_COUNT.

testQuestionSettings.description: **OPTIONAL**: Test question configuration. jobId and teamId must match main job.

timeLimitThresholdSec.description: **OPTIONAL**: Time limit threshold as percentage (0-100), not seconds. Default: 0 (disabled).

enableExternalAutoAssignJob.description: **OPTIONAL**: Enable external auto assignment. Default: false.

enableExpirationTime.description: **OPTIONAL**: Expiration control. Default: 0 (disabled). When >0, duration fields must be >0.

qaAdvancedOptions.description: **OPTIONAL**: QA advanced configuration options. Default: null.

alias.description: **READ-ONLY**: Job alias (auto-generated like W837054 or Q837054).

running.description: **READ-ONLY**: Whether job is currently running (computed field).

draft.description: **READ-ONLY**: Whether job is in draft state (computed field).

appenConnectCrowd.description: **READ-ONLY**: Whether using Appen Connect crowd (computed field).

multipleJudgement.description: **READ-ONLY**: Whether multiple judgments enabled (computed from judgmentsPerRow > 1).

enableDynamicJudgements.description: Enable dynamic judgment adjustment. Default: false.

matchingJudgements.description: Required matching judgments. Min: 1 when used.

enabledQuestions.description: JSON string of enabled questions configuration.

UploadErrorCodes.description: Comprehensive list of error codes for the upload internal contributors API

4001.description: File content is required - Input stream is null

4002.description: File name is required - fileName parameter is null or empty

4003.description: Invalid teamId format - teamId parameter is not in valid UUID format

4501.description: Failed to get upload URL - Internal service error when generating presigned URL

4502.description: Failed to upload file to S3 - Error during file upload to S3 storage

4503.description: Failed to create batch job - Error when creating batch job for file processing

4504.description: Failed to activate batch job - Error when activating the created batch job

4505.description: File uploaded successfully but processing failed - General processing exception

4506.description: Internal server error - Unexpected server error during processing

4507.description: File processing failed - File processing returned FAILED, ERROR, INVALID, REJECTED status or timed out

4508.description: Failed to get final file status - Error when retrieving final processing results

tags.name: Projects
tags.description:
Project management operations for organizing and structuring your data annotation workflows.

- **Project Structure:**
  - Projects serve as containers for organizing related jobs and data
  - Each project belongs to a specific team and can contain multiple jobs
  - Projects provide a logical grouping for workflow management

- **Key Operations:**
  - **Create Projects**: Initialize new project containers with a name.

- **Project Components:**
  - **Jobs**: Individual annotation tasks within a project
  - **Units**: Data rows uploaded to projects for annotation
  - **Judgments**: Contributor responses collected on project units

- **Project Lifecycle:**
  1. **Creation**: Initialize project with name and description
  2. **Configuration**: Set up project settings and access controls
  3. **Population**: Add jobs, upload units, and configure workflows
  4. **Execution**: Run jobs and collect judgments
  5. **Analysis**: Generate reports and analyze results

- **Integration Points:**
  - **Job Management**: Create and manage jobs within projects
  - **Data Upload**: Upload units and datasets to projects
  - **Unit Operations**: List, filter, and manage project units
  - **Workflow Routing**: Send units between jobs within project
  - **Reporting**: Generate project/job-level analytics and reports

tags.name: Upload
tags.description:
File upload operations for importing datasets and data into your projects and jobs.

- **Dataset Upload Process:**
  - **Upload Files**: Send CSV files containing your data to existing projects
  - **Status Monitoring**: Track upload progress and completion status
  - **File Validation**: Automatic validation of CSV format, headers, and content

- **Supported File Formats:**
  - **CSV Files**: Primary format for data import
  - **Encoding**: UTF-8 encoding recommended
  - **Headers**: Required column headers for data organization
  - **Data Types**: Text, numeric, date, and structured data support

- **Upload Workflow:**
  1. **File Preparation**: Ensure CSV format with proper headers
  2. **Upload Initiation**: Send file to project with metadata
  3. **Processing**: System validates and processes the data
  4. **Status Polling**: Monitor status until completion
  5. **Data Integration**: Units become available for job assignments

- **Key Features:**
  - **Batch Processing**: Handle large datasets efficiently
  - **Progress Tracking**: Real-time upload status monitoring
  - **Error Handling**: Detailed validation error messages
  - **File Management**: Track uploaded files and their metadata

- **Integration Points:**
  - **Project Association**: Uploads are linked to specific projects
  - **Unit Creation**: Each row becomes a unit for annotation
  - **Job Routing**: Units can be sent to jobs for contributor work

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description:
Job management operations for creating, configuring, and controlling data annotation workflows.

- **Job Lifecycle Management:**
  - **Create Jobs**: Initialize new annotation tasks with project association and type specification
  - **Update Settings**: Modify job configuration, instructions, and workflow parameters
  - **Launch Jobs**: Activate jobs from DRAFT to RUNNING state for contributor access
  - **Pause Jobs**: Temporarily halt job execution while preserving progress

- **Job Types Supported:**
  - **WORK**: Standard annotation jobs for tasks
  - **QA**: Quality assurance jobs for reviewing, validating work and providing the feedback.

- **Job States:**
  1. **DRAFT**: Initial creation state, configurable but not accessible to contributors
  2. **RUNNING**: Active state where contributors can work on assignments
  3. **PAUSED**: Temporarily halted, preserving all progress and judgments

tags.name: Contributor Access URL Construction
tags.description:
## Contributor Access URL Construction

With this release, customers can now generate the Contributor Access URL programmatically.

### URL Structure
```
https://account.appen.com/quality/tasks/{jobId}?secret={secret}
```

### Components
- **Base URL**: `https://account.appen.com/quality/tasks/` (constant string)
- **Job ID**: Returned in the response of the Create Job API (example: `2d215241-00bc-4453-bac3-449387a78190`)
- **Secret**: Returned in the response of the Update Job Settings API (example: `xicPrChRfj28e7GLZiDykuTDQZqpJYv6QkBRLacU5bp4eE`)

### Example Construction
```
https://account.appen.com/quality/tasks/2d215241-00bc-4453-bac3-449387a78190?secret=xicPrChRfj28e7GLZiDykuTDQZqpJYv6QkBRLacU5bp4eE
```

tags.name: Route Units
tags.description:
Unit management operations for handling individual data rows and their distribution across jobs.

- **Unit Operations:**
  - **List Units**: Retrieve and filter units within a project with pagination capabilities
  - **Send Units to Job**: Route units from projects to specific jobs for annotation work
  - **Unit Filtering**: Filter units by job status, dataset row type, and other criteria
  - **Bulk Operations**: Handle multiple units simultaneously for efficient workflow management

- **Unit Types:**
  - **Individual Units**: Single data rows ready for annotation
  - **Unit Groups**: Collections of related units that can be processed together
  - **Dataset Rows**: Structured data from uploaded CSV files

- **Key Features:**
  - **Advanced Filtering**: Filter by job status, dataset type
  - **Pagination Support**: Handle large datasets with efficient pagination
  - **Sorting Options**: Customizable sorting for better data organization
  - **Status Tracking**: Monitor unit status across different job assignments

- **Unit Lifecycle Management:**
  1. **Creation**: Units are created when data is uploaded to projects
  2. **Assignment**: Units are distributed to jobs for contributor work
  3. **Processing**: Contributors annotate units and submit judgments
  4. **Routing**: Units can be moved between jobs (e.g., work to QA)
  5. **Completion**: Units receive required judgments and are marked complete

tags.name: Download
tags.description:
Generate and download reports for either a single job or an entire project, and download datasets from projects.

- **Choose scope with exactly one of:**
  - jobId: download a Job-level report
  - projectId: download a Project-level report or dataset
  (Do not send both.)

- **Required query parameters:**
  - type: the report you want to generate or dataset to download
  - filename: the output file name (e.g., report.csv)

- **How it works:**
  - When you trigger the API for the first time, the API returns 202 (in progress).
  - **You must call the same endpoint again with the exact same query parameters** to check the status. Repeat the request every 2-5 seconds until you receive a 200 response.
  - When the report is ready for download, the API returns 200 and streams the file directly in the response body.
  - **No separate URL fetch is needed** - the file content is streamed directly when ready (200 response).
  - The file is automatically saved to the path specified in your curl `-o` flag or your API tool's save location. 

- **Supported report types:**
  **Job-level (use jobId):**
    - full: All individual judgments
    - json: JSON report with aggregated and raw judgments
    - aggregated: Aggregated results (requires request body; see below)
    - workset: Contributor (Workset) report
    - source: Original source data uploaded to the job
    - ai_detector_report_contributor_level
    - ai_detector_report_unit_level
    - test_question_report_contributor_level
    - k_alpha_report: Inter-annotator agreement (Krippendorff’s alpha)
    - contributor_scores_report: Contributor Scores Report

  **Project-level (use projectId):**
    - job_daily_report_project_level
    - contributor_report_project_level
    - source_report_project_level
    - json_report_project_level

  **Dataset Download (use projectId):**
    - DATA_SET: Unit View dataset with customizable unit selection and column filtering
    - JUDGMENT_DATA_SET: Judgment View dataset with customizable unit selection and column filtering
    - Supports filtering by unit IDs and column selection
    - Optional filter support with filterName and userId/akonId parameters

- **Aggregated reports (type=aggregated, Job-level only):**
  - Provide a request body with aggregation rules for every judgment column:
    judgmentColumnAggregation: [
      { judgmentColumn, aggregationAlgorithm, parameter? }
    ]
  - aggregationAlgorithm must be one of:
    - AGG (Best Answer)
    - AGG_X (Top # Answers) → requires parameter.numberOfAnswers
    - CAGG_X (Confidence Score) → requires parameter.minimumConfidence (0–1)
    - ALL (All Answers)
    - AVG (Numeric Average)
  - Example: choose one algorithm per column (e.g., "_meta", "ask_question_here").

- **File format:**
  - Most types return CSV files; type=json returns a JSON file.
  - The final file name uses the provided filename parameter.

- **Authentication:**
  - Authorization: Token token=your-api-key

- **Responses:**
  - 200: DONE with downloadableUrl (and Location header)
  - 202: Report generation in progress
  - 400/403/404/500: Error with details

tags.name: Test Questions
tags.description:
Test question management operations for evaluating contributor performance and ensuring quality assurance in data annotation workflows.

- **Key Operations:**
  - **Add Test Questions**: Create new test questions and link them to specific units
  - **Upload Test Questions**: Bulk upload test questions from CSV files
  - **Update Test Questions**: Modify existing test questions and its settings

- **Test Question Features:**
  - **Enable/Disable**: Control whether test questions are active for contributor evaluation
  - **Hide/Show**: Manage visibility of test question answers and explanation
  - **Content Management**: Update question content, answers, and explanations
  - **Mode Configuration**: Set QUIZ, WORK, or QUIZ+WORK modes for different evaluation types
  - **Performance Tracking**: Monitor contributor scores metrics
  - **Calibration**: Ensure consistent annotation quality across different contributors

- **Authentication:**
  - Authorization: Token token=your-api-key

- **Common Responses:**
  - 200: Success with test question data
  - 400: Bad request - Invalid parameters or duplicate test questions
  - 403: Access denied
  - 404: Project, job, or test question not found
  - 500: Internal server error

unitId.description: Unique identifier of the unit containing the test question to update

jobId.description: Unique identifier of the job that contains the test question

enableDisableMode.description: Whether the test question is enabled for contributor evaluation

hideAnswerMode.description: Whether to hide the test question answers from contributors

quizWorkMode.description: The mode in which the test question operates

minimumMatchingAccuracy.description: Minimum matching accuracy threshold as a percentage (0-100)

questions.description: Array of test questions with their answers and explanations

answer.description: Array of correct answers for the test question

reason.description: Array of explanations for each answer
